scope_in: PDPA + BoT consent / lawful basis for displaying customer balances
Data minimization in proposed response shape
Audit-trail design (BoT IT-Risk requires every read recorded, including cache hit)
scope_out: Full STRIDE threat model — deferred to post-code-review gate
SAST / DAST / SCA / container scans — no code yet
OWASP ASVS L2 full traversal — deferred to full security gate

lawful_basis_recommendation.rationale: Balance inquiry is an inherent right of the deposit account holder under the existing customer agreement; consistent with money-transfer's documented lawful basis (contract performance); no new personal data collected; no profiling / marketing / secondary use; fresh §19 consent would be redundant and legally over-engineered.
alternatives_considered_and_rejected: PDPA §19 fresh consent — rejected: redundant with contractual basis, UX friction
PDPA §24(5) legitimate interest — rejected: weaker than §24(3); §24(3) already covers

purpose_limitation_check.money_transfer_purpose: execute funds transfers + view account info to complete transfers
purpose_limitation_check.verdict: Both purposes within the same overarching deposit-account-relationship scope; no purpose-stretch
purpose_limitation_check.caveat: Privacy notice text must explicitly cover 'balance inquiry via digital channels' before GA — see condition C-1

audit_trail_check.highest_risk_status: EXPLICITLY COVERED by BA — exceeds requirement
coverage: audit on success (cache hit + cache miss)
audit on excluded accounts (separate observability counter)

hardening_findings: S-EARLY-001 (low) — audit event payload must be metadata-only (no raw balance)
S-EARLY-002 (low) — customerId in structured logs should be pseudonymized or documented as internal-only
S-EARLY-003 (info) — explicitly forbid PII in URL / query string / Prometheus metric labels

enforcement_layers: API Gateway: JWT validated, X-Customer-Id header injected from JWT sub
BDS service: assert X-Customer-Id == JWT sub (mismatch -> 403 + audit FORBIDDEN)
Service-level filter: AccountClient call derives customerId from JWT sub only
idor_check.strengthening_required: C-3 — JWT sub is single source of truth; X-Customer-Id header is treated as untrusted input used only for mismatch detection, never for business logic or downstream calls. Pattern to be enforced via CustomerIdResolver + static analysis rule.

hard_rules_preflight.secrets_in_artifacts: PASS — grep verified zero secrets in BA + PM docs
hard_rules_preflight.pii_in_logs: MITIGATED — findings S-EARLY-001/002/003 cover audit / logs / metric labels
hard_rules_preflight.money_in_float_double: DEFER — TL/BE; BigDecimal inherited from AccountInfo (money-transfer)
hard_rules_preflight.missing_audit_event: EXCEEDS — read endpoint but BA still audits every read incl. cache hit
hard_rules_preflight.hs256_jwt_prod: INHERITED — money-transfer ITEM-2 mandates RS256 + Vault
hard_rules_preflight.unauthenticated_customer_data_endpoint: PASS — NFR §3 + AC-001-E3 + feature flag default false
hard_rules_preflight.no_bypass_of_audit_trail: EXPLICITLY PASS — BR-14 forbids cache short-circuit of audit

findings.title: Audit event payload must be metadata-only — no raw balance value retained for 7 years
findings.description: BA implies audit payload contains only metadata, but does not explicitly forbid balance/accountNumber in payload. Without explicit prohibition, implementers might include accounts[] for traceability — creating a 7-year retained balance-history honeypot.
findings.remediation: Make EXPLICIT in audit event JSON schema: payload contains ONLY {eventType, actorId, customerId, purpose, channel, correlationId, timestamp, result, accountCount, cacheHit}. MUST NOT include accounts[], balance, accountNumber, balance_as_of per-account, currency. Add integration test asserting payload absence of these fields.

findings.title: customerId in structured logs — pseudonymize or document as internal-only
findings.location: NFR §6 Observability — structured log JSON schema
findings.description: BA includes customerId UUID in INFO-level structured logs. While not strictly PII (UUID is internal pseudonym), aggregation systems (Elasticsearch / Loki / Splunk) retain it as a long-lived linkable identifier consistent with money-transfer Finding S-08.
findings.remediation: Either (a) hash customerId for log appearance (HMAC + rotating key), keeping raw value only in audit-service events, OR (b) explicitly document customerId UUID is treated as 'internal pseudonym' acceptable in internal logs but NEVER in external-facing logs / metrics / URLs. Pick one and document in ADR.

findings.title: Make explicit: no PII in URL / query string / Prometheus metric labels
findings.description: BA does not explicitly forbid customerId in URL path/query string or high-cardinality metric labels. Current design uses header (good) but the rule should be explicit to prevent future drift.
findings.remediation: Add to OpenAPI spec + metric design doc: (a) customerId MUST NOT appear in URL path or query string; (b) Prometheus metric labels MUST be low-cardinality (status, cache_hit, cache_miss_reason) — NEVER customerId / accountId values.

findings.title: Make JWT sub the structurally enforced single source of truth for customerId
findings.description: Current design has 3 enforcement layers (gateway, BDS guard, service filter). However, the BDS guard 'assert X-Customer-Id == JWT sub' must trust JWT sub as source of truth, not the header. Without a structural constraint (CustomerIdResolver bean + static analysis rule), a future PR could silently switch the source from JWT sub to header.
findings.remediation: C-3 — TL defines CustomerIdResolver pattern; X-Customer-Id header is only for IDOR-mismatch detection, never as the value for business logic or downstream calls. Add Spotbugs/PMD rule or unit test detecting direct request.getHeader('X-Customer-Id') outside the IDOR-check filter.

findings.title: Verify customer-facing privacy notice covers 'balance inquiry via digital channels'
findings.location: External — customer onboarding privacy notice (compliance team scope)
findings.description: If onboarding privacy notice was scoped narrowly to 'transaction execution', dashboard read might be a documented-purpose stretch even though lawful basis (§24(3)) is sound. Demo on synthetic users is unaffected.
findings.remediation: C-1 — SA coordinates with compliance team to verify notice text. If gap found, schedule notice update BEFORE GA (post-demo). Out of agent scope to update the notice itself.

findings.title: Verify shared Redis cluster supports AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption
findings.description: BA states Redis at-rest encryption required (AES-256-GCM consistent with account-service). Need to verify the SHARED cluster actually supports it; if not, ASSUME-004 is invalidated.
findings.remediation: C-4 — SA + DevOps verify cluster encryption posture; if not enabled, either enable on shared cluster OR provision dedicated namespace. Document in ADR.

compliance_items_for_sa_tl.title: Privacy notice verification (GA-gated, not demo-blocking)
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.action: Coordinate with compliance team to verify customer onboarding privacy notice explicitly covers 'balance inquiry via digital / mobile banking channels'. If gap → schedule notice update BEFORE GA.
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.demo_impact: NONE — synthetic test users; not blocking sprint demo 2026-06-04

compliance_items_for_sa_tl.title: Audit event payload — metadata only
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.action: Make EXPLICIT in audit event JSON schema that payload contains ONLY metadata (no raw balance, no accountNumber, no balance_as_of per-account). Add integration test asserting payload absence of these fields.
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.demo_impact: Must be in design before BE implementation starts

compliance_items_for_sa_tl.title: JWT sub = structurally enforced single source of truth for customerId
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.action: Define CustomerIdResolver bean / static helper. X-Customer-Id header is treated as untrusted input — used only for IDOR-mismatch detection, never as the value for business logic or downstream calls. Add static-analysis rule (Spotbugs/PMD) or unit test detecting direct header usage outside the IDOR-check filter.
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.demo_impact: Must be in TL implementation notes before BE coding

compliance_items_for_sa_tl.title: Verify Redis cluster at-rest encryption capability
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.action: Verify shared Redis cluster (ASSUME-004) supports AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption. If not, enable on shared cluster OR provision dedicated namespace. Document in ADR. If neither feasible, escalate (NFR §3 + ASSUME-004 invalidated).
compliance_items_for_sa_tl.demo_impact: Must be verified before code touches Redis with PII cache payload

risk_003_mitigation_status.rationale: Consent reuse verified under PDPA §24(3) contractual basis (same as money-transfer); audit-trail design exceeds requirement (BR-14 audit-on-cache-hit); 4 conditions tracked for SA/TL. Likelihood drops from medium-low (assumed) to low (verified). Impact unchanged (regulatory). PM should downgrade in next risk-register update.

pci_dss.rationale: No PAN, CVV, track data, or expiry. Service reads deposit-account balances only. Inherits money-transfer's OUT-OF-SCOPE posture.
controls_met: Req-4 no card data in transit

gdpr.lawful_basis: Contract performance (PDPA §24(3) equivalent to GDPR Art 6(1)(b))
gdpr.retention: Audit events 7 years (BoT mandated); response payload not persisted; Redis cache TTL 30s; balance data sourced from account-service / ledger-service governed by their existing retention
gdpr.data_subject_rights: Inherited — no new collection beyond money-transfer. Erasure follows existing legal-hold-override policy (7y BoT retention).
gdpr.data_residency: Thailand only (PDPA + BoT cross-border rules) — inherited K8s topology constraint from money-transfer ITEM-9

pdpa.consent_management: Out of scope for this service — operates under existing banking contract; consent registry handled by identity-service per PDPA §19
pdpa.data_minimization: Response shape verified minimal (NFR §8); audit / log / metric strengthening tracked via S-EARLY-001/002/003
pdpa.purpose_limitation: purpose='balance-inquiry' tagged on every audit event; within same customer-relationship scope as money-transfer

bot_it_risk.audit_logging: PASS — audit on every read incl. cache hit, append-only, 7y retention, async non-blocking
bot_it_risk.data_residency: Thailand only — inherited from money-transfer K8s topology constraint
bot_it_risk.segregation_of_duties: Inherited — ops staff cannot modify audit records

conditions: C-1: Privacy notice verification (GA-gated; not demo-blocking) — owner banking-solution-architect
C-2: Audit event payload = metadata-only (no raw balance / accountNumber) — owner banking-tech-lead
C-3: JWT sub = structurally enforced single source of truth for customerId; CustomerIdResolver pattern + static analysis rule — owner banking-tech-lead + banking-backend-dev
C-4: Verify Redis cluster supports AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption — owner banking-solution-architect + banking-devops
review_areas_completed: BoT IT-Risk audit alignment (incl. audit-on-cache-hit verification)
Data minimization findings (response shape + audit payload + logs + metrics)
Banking hard-rule pre-flight (11 auto-fail rules + extras)
metadata.next_security_touchpoint: Full SAST/DAST/STRIDE gate post code-review (D9) per workflow.md — banking-security re-engaged after banking-reviewer-be + banking-reviewer-fe both approve
metadata.demo_posture: ACCEPTABLE on synthetic test users; GA requires C-1 verification
metadata.notes: Early-look shift-left review per RISK-003 mitigation plan. Returns to banking-player (orchestrator) — not banking-qa — because this is NOT the standard pre-QA security gate. Player should: (1) downgrade RISK-003 in risk register, (2) forward conditions C-2/C-3/C-4 to banking-solution-architect + banking-tech-lead in their respective handoffs, (3) record C-1 as post-sprint follow-up. Full STRIDE / SAST / DAST gate runs later at D9.
